You start thinking this is a casual relaxing game. That changes quickly.

BOR ... RRRRRING!!! These build to budget games are all the same ... Impossible with what you are given after starting off very promisingly. Also on a number of occasions there have been different results with the same bridge design. Flawed game physics could be what this proves. Nope - this is crap!

This game is so relaxing and so does the soundtrack. It'll be a perfect fit for Saturday morning short gaming session.

I Like This.

VERY basic.  You get to build bridges out of wood, steel, steel cables, and occasionally a concrete pillar.  Most levels you only get to use 1 or 2 of those materials.  You have very few options for attachment points.

Remember that scene from Margin Call in which Stanley Tucci calculates how many hours of human life he saved by building a bridge in Ohio?

My career in Bridge Constructor is basically that, but the complete opposite. I've not only lost 22 hours of my life, I've also blown up about 37,000 cars, trucks, and tanker trunks.

I've made peace with it.

Rating: Fun

It has been made redundant because of Poly Bridge which is a better game in every way.

Bridge constructor is a physics puzzle game with a ton of different levels to solve. This game is genuinely fantastic for any age. Whether you want to create mayhem for the big rig, or calculate the perfect counterweight to maintain a ridiculous bridge, this game has you covered. Personally, I was surprised at just how well made and fun this game actually is. Definitely recommend picking this one up if you enjoy solving problems.

It's okay; gameplay is fun & actually makes you think but the graphic can be off-putting after looking at it for awhile (i.e. the graphics did not age well) and the sound effects & music can be irritating.

I remember enjoying the original Bridge Builder game a lot back in the day and I love various other "engineering" games so I thought that this would be right up my alley. Sadly I was utterly disappointed. The mediocre presentation doesn't matter but the terrible UI makes it far less enjoyable than it should be. My main gripe, though, is that the game at the same time imposes very strict limitations on how many parts you can use AND does not provide tutorials for learning effective patterns and such which makes finding solutions a chore. Come on, even if my bridge goes over budget, at least let me see if it *would* work so I can learn.

In terms of bridge building games I suppose this is as refined as it gets. You have just 4 materials, wood, steel, concrete and wires. The game is split into different islands, and each island consists of around 8 bridges to build. In general the complexity and difficulty goes up by each bridge you build. Different levels limit which materials you can use and there is always a budget to stick under adding a very challenging element to the game (especially trying to get the heavier vehicles across the bridge too). 

Overall I thought the game was pretty good, and the levels were well designed. My only gripe is personally being fairly new to any sort of bridge building game, I would have liked a little bit more of a tutorial explaining how things worked, especially the wires. I did get stuck on a fair few levels just trying to figure out how they worked by trial and error.

Otherwise a solid little game, received for free, and had some fun with it so nothing really to complain about.
Good light weight bridge game. I enjoy building bridges good enough to get the test vehicles across but which collapse immediately after.

Overall: I honestly enjoyed this great game.
It is a very relaxing experience to play with.
And I just don't have any negatives to say.

A game like this lives or dies on its physics engine, and fortunately this feels realistic enough. On each stage you must construct a bridge using limited materials; once you have finished and the trucks start to cross it you can see where the supports are coming under strain, and there's a nice bit of tension as you will them to not snap. It's never going to set the world alight, but it's a good package and well worth spending a few hours playing with.
Poor man's Poly Bridge.

Fun game, but it would benefit greatly from having explanations of each type of bridge that you can use as a basis for understanding what it is you're doing. You can find such information in various places online or just use the walkthrus for a bit and you'll pick it up. 

Otherwise, the game is punishingly difficult until you do learn those basics, and then it's mostly a matter of picking which type of bridge is best and then modifying it a bit. You can't "wing it" on most of them.

This is a mobile ported bridge constructor game/physics puzzle game where you must use a variety of materials and engineering structures to create sturdy bridges over chasms. 

In a surprise to nobody, we once more see greedy mobile devs trying to screw over PC gamers.  On Steam, this is $10 USD, on app stores, it's $2 USD.   Mobile devs must learn PC gamers are not here to be gouged, and can't be expected to pay a premium for a mobile game just because it's been lazily dumped on Steam.

the level design is incredibly lazy, the entire game can be beaten with three bridge designs and within 2 hours (yes, i beat the game in this time)
if you don't want to buy poly bridge, at least save yourself some dignity and buy bridge contructor portal.

Nice little bridgebuilder, with solutions that can be found without endless trial and error.

This is by far my favorite bridge construction game that I've found so far. It's not perfect but I enjoyed myself the entire time. It's challenging but not too difficult. Unlike Polybridge the bridges are straight forward - get the vehicle to the other side. Polybridge requires you to do all kinds of ridiculous "stunts" and complex machines; imo it's too hard to the point of not being fun. This is also the only bridge constructor I've played that feels like the outcome isn't completely chaotic. A small tweak isn't going to cause vastly different behavior; it's very easy to tweak your bridge to perfection once you get the general layout. And if you want to do ridiculous stuff it's very possible! I personally like having bridges collapse but still earn a victory. 

Bottom line: if you want to get vehicles over bridges with some solid, simple mechanics this is the bridge builder for you.

Very fun bridge builder game, with one major downfall. A lot of the levels in this game require inventive thinking and lots of trial and error, which can both be fun. But at a certain point your bridges need to withstand such a heavy payload that they are no longer that, but instead most people will realistically need to be looking up guides on the internet for specific builds to copy over. This isn't the case for all levels, but it certainly is for a lot. What I'm saying is quite a few of these levels will get so difficult, that there are really two or three very specific ways to complete them at most, which eliminates a lot of personal creativity and fun to be honest. 

At least this was all my experience. Take this review with a grain of salt, but definitely expect this game to be something along these lines. I wanted to recommend this game, and very nearly did, but in the end the guide-seeking and build-copying just outweighed the fun for me by a little bit. If you don't care for looking up a few guides however, or if you believe you can handle it yourself without it, then go pick this game up! Especially on sale, Bridge Constructor can really be worth it otherwise.

So, the original Bridge Constructor is a good game on its own, but you really should start here, and then play the others, because after the excitement of the others, the vanilla game feels a bit too, well, vanilla.

This game though, does throw a lot of curve balls at you. First of all, after you've completed every bridge (on the base campaign) with the normal cars and trucks (fortunately, getting the truck across counts for cars as well), you unlock a "hard mode" of having to cross the bridge with a tanker truck, which is much harder. The bonus maps (Easter, Halloween and Christmas) have these tanker trucks replaced by appropriate holiday vehicles, which is a nice touch.

It does get tedious, I'm not going to lie. Most of the normal bridges just operate on the principle of "triangles, triangles and triangles", but the bonus ones seem to need some very strange solutions. I did have to look up a number of them, and one of them even involved having the vehicles swing across the gap, which didn't seem possible, but there you are.

The experience was worth the few dollars I paid for it, but it dragged a bit near the end.
